# google_contact_center_insights_qa_question

A single question to be scored by the Insights QA feature.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=contact_center_insights_qa_question_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Contact Center Insights Qa Question Basic


```hcl
resource "google_contact_center_insights_qa_scorecard" "scorecard" {
  qa_scorecard_id = "%{scorecard_id}"
  location        = "us-central1"
  display_name    = "My Scorecard"
  source          = "QA_SCORECARD_SOURCE_CUSTOMER_DEFINED"
}

resource "google_contact_center_insights_qa_scorecard_revision" "rev" {
  qa_scorecard = google_contact_center_insights_qa_scorecard.scorecard.qa_scorecard_id
  location     = "us-central1"
}

resource "google_contact_center_insights_qa_question" "default" {
  qa_scorecard    = google_contact_center_insights_qa_scorecard.scorecard.qa_scorecard_id
  revision       = google_contact_center_insights_qa_scorecard_revision.rev.qa_scorecard_revision_id
  location       = "us-central1"

  question_body  = "Did the agent greet the customer?"
  question_type  = "CUSTOMIZABLE"

  answer_choices {
    str_value = "Yes"
    score     = 1.0
  }
  answer_choices {
    str_value = "No"
    score     = 0.5
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `qa_scorecard` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `revision` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.


* `abbreviation` -
  (Optional)
  Short, descriptive string, used in the UI where it's not practical
  to display the full question body. E.g., "Greeting".

* `answer_choices` -
  (Optional)
  A list of valid answers to the question, which the LLM must choose from.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_answer_choices).

* `answer_instructions` -
  (Optional)
  Instructions describing how to determine the answer.

* `metrics` -
  (Optional)
  A wrapper representing metrics calculated against a test-set on a LLM that
  was fine tuned for this question.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_metrics).

* `order` -
  (Optional)
  Defines the order of the question within its parent scorecard revision.

* `predefined_question_config` -
  (Optional)
  Configuration for a predefined question. This field will only be set if the
  Question Type is predefined.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_predefined_question_config).

* `qa_question_data_options` -
  (Optional)
  Options for configuring the data used to generate the QA question.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_qa_question_data_options).

* `question_body` -
  (Optional)
  Question text. E.g., "Did the agent greet the customer?"

* `question_type` -
  (Optional)
  The type of question.
  Possible values:
  CUSTOMIZABLE
  PREDEFINED

* `tags` -
  (Optional)
  Questions are tagged for categorization and scoring. Tags can either be:
  - Default Tags: These are predefined categories. They are identified by
  their string value (e.g., "BUSINESS", "COMPLIANCE", and "CUSTOMER").
  - Custom Tags: These are user-defined categories. They are identified by
  their full resource name (e.g.,
  projects/{project}/locations/{location}/qaQuestionTags/{qa_question_tag}).
  Both default and custom tags are used to group questions and to influence
  the scoring of each question.

* `tuning_metadata` -
  (Optional)
  Metadata about the tuning operation for the question. Will only be set if a
  scorecard containing this question has been tuned.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_tuning_metadata).

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.



<a name="nested_answer_choices"></a>The `answer_choices` block supports:

* `bool_value` -
  (Optional)
  Boolean value.

* `key` -
  (Optional)
  A short string used as an identifier.

* `na_value` -
  (Optional)
  A value of "Not Applicable (N/A)". If provided, this field may only
  be set to `true`. If a question receives this answer, it will be
  excluded from any score calculations.

* `num_value` -
  (Optional)
  Numerical value.

* `score` -
  (Optional)
  Numerical score of the answer, used for generating the overall score of
  a QaScorecardResult. If the answer uses na_value, this field is unused.

* `str_value` -
  (Optional)
  String value.

<a name="nested_metrics"></a>The `metrics` block supports:

* `accuracy` -
  (Output)
  Accuracy of the model. Measures the percentage of correct answers the
  model gave on the test set.

<a name="nested_predefined_question_config"></a>The `predefined_question_config` block supports:

* `type` -
  (Optional)
  The type of the predefined question.
  Possible values:
  CONVERSATION_OUTCOME
  CONVERSATION_OUTCOME_ESCALATION_INITIATOR_ROLE

<a name="nested_qa_question_data_options"></a>The `qa_question_data_options` block supports:

* `conversation_data_options` -
  (Optional)
  Options for configuring what metadata is included in the conversation data
  used in QAI and Discovery Engine.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_qa_question_data_options_conversation_data_options).


<a name="nested_qa_question_data_options_conversation_data_options"></a>The `conversation_data_options` block supports:

* `include_dialogflow_interaction_data` -
  (Optional)
  Whether to include the per turn Dialogflow interaction data in conversation
  transcript.

<a name="nested_tuning_metadata"></a>The `tuning_metadata` block supports:

* `dataset_validation_warnings` -
  (Optional)
  A list of any applicable data validation warnings about the question's
  feedback labels.

* `total_valid_label_count` -
  (Optional)
  Total number of valid labels provided for the question at the time of
  tuining.

* `tuning_error` -
  (Optional)
  Error status of the tuning operation for the question. Will only be set
  if the tuning operation failed.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/qaScorecards/{{qa_scorecard}}/revisions/{{revision}}/qaQuestions/{{name}}`

* `create_time` -
  The time at which this question was created.

* `name` -
  Identifier. The resource name of the question.
  Format:
  projects/{project}/locations/{location}/qaScorecards/{qa_scorecard}/revisions/{revision}/qaQuestions/{qa_question}

* `update_time` -
  The most recent time at which the question was updated.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


QaQuestion can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/qaScorecards/{{qa_scorecard}}/revisions/{{revision}}/qaQuestions/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{qa_scorecard}}/{{revision}}/{{name}}`
* `{{location}}/{{qa_scorecard}}/{{revision}}/{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/identities) to import QaQuestion using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-optional value->"
    location = "<-required value->"
    qaScorecard = "<-required value->"
    revision = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_contact_center_insights_qa_question.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import QaQuestion using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/qaScorecards/{{qa_scorecard}}/revisions/{{revision}}/qaQuestions/{{name}}"
  to = google_contact_center_insights_qa_question.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), QaQuestion can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_contact_center_insights_qa_question.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/qaScorecards/{{qa_scorecard}}/revisions/{{revision}}/qaQuestions/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_contact_center_insights_qa_question.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{qa_scorecard}}/{{revision}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_contact_center_insights_qa_question.default {{location}}/{{qa_scorecard}}/{{revision}}/{{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
